title: 10 Herbal Creams For Ringworm
intro_desc: Herbal creams for ringworm are a popular alternative to traditional treatments. They work by using the antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties of certain herbs to soothe and heal the affected skin. One of the main reasons herbal creams can relieve ringworm is that they promote a healthy environment for the skin to heal. For example, Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has been shown to have antifungal properties that can help combat the fungus that causes ringworm. This means that the cream can help to clear up the infection and prevent it from spreading.  Calendula officinalis, or marigold, is another herb that's often used in ringworm creams. It's known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and itching associated with ringworm. By using a cream that contains calendula, you can help to calm the skin and promote healing. Eucalyptus globulus, or eucalyptus, is also sometimes used in ringworm creams. It has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a useful addition to a treatment cream.  Using herbal creams for ringworm can bring several benefits to your life. For one, they can be a more natural and gentle alternative to traditional treatments, which can be harsh and irritating to the skin. They can also be a more cost-effective option, as they often don't require a prescription or expensive medical visits. Additionally, herbal creams can be used in conjunction with other treatments, such as antifungal medications or medicated shampoos, to help clear up the infection more quickly. This means that you may be able to get rid of ringworm faster and with fewer side effects.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains compounds like cineole, limonene, and terpinen-4-ol, which have antifungal properties that help combat ringworm. These essential oils work by disrupting the fungal cell membrane, ultimately leading to the death of the fungus causing the infection. Terpinen-4-ol, in particular, has been shown to have potent antifungal activity against a wide range of fungi, including those that cause ringworm. Cineole and limonene also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin affected by the infection. By using a cream made from Melaleuca alternifolia, people may be able to effectively treat and manage ringworm infections.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Stir in the distilled water, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container. Let it cool and harden completely before use.
Apply a thin layer of the cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day. Repeat for 2-3 weeks or until the ringworm clears up.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoids, flavonoids, and carotenoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm the skin. The sesquiterpenes in Calendula officinalis creams, such as isohamnenol and isohamnenone, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause ringworm. The antiseptic and antifungal properties of the sesquiterpenes help to reduce the severity and duration of ringworm symptoms. By applying Calendula officinalis creams to the affected area, you can reduce inflammation, kill fungi, and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Calendula officinalis petals, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil.
Infuse the Calendula petals in the coconut oil by placing them in a saucepan and heating it over low heat for 2-3 hours. Strain the mixture and discard the petals.
In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax over low heat. Add the infused oil, vitamin E oil, and lavender oil to the melted beeswax.
Stir the mixture until it's smooth and combined. Remove it from heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick consistency.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once it's set, use it to treat ringworm by applying it 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains compounds like eucalyptol and cineole, which have antifungal properties that help fight fungal infections like ringworm. These properties also reduce inflammation and promote healing. The antibacterial properties of eucalyptol and cineole help prevent bacterial infections that can lead to further complications. Additionally, eucalyptol's antiseptic properties help clean the affected area and prevent the spread of the infection. The soothing properties of eucalyptus globulus creams also help to calm irritated skin and promote a healthy environment for healing.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a small saucepan.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax over low heat, stirring until the beeswax dissolves.
Add 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus oil and 1 tablespoon of tea tree oil to the melted mixture, and stir well.
Remove the saucepan from heat and stir in 2 tablespoons of shea butter until it's fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a small container, let it cool and solidify, then apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams contains bioactive constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, which have antifungal properties. These compounds help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, such as Microsporum canis and Trichophyton mentagrophytes. The gel from the aloe vera plant also contains vitamins A, C, and E, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the affected skin. The antiseptic and antifungal properties of aloe vera help to reduce the severity of ringworm symptoms, including redness, itching, and scaling. When applied topically, aloe vera creams can help to speed up the healing process of ringworm by promoting a healthy environment for skin repair.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of tea tree oil.
Melt the beeswax in a saucepan over low heat. Add the coconut oil and stir until melted.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes. Add the aloe vera gel and stir until combined.
Add the tea tree oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a bowl and let it cool and solidify completely.
Once solid, use a spoon or spatula to mix the cream until smooth and creamy. Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Allium sativum creams contains a compound called allicin, which has antifungal properties that help fight ringworm. The sulfur compounds in Allium sativum, including diallyl disulfide and diallyl trisulfide, also contribute to its antifungal activity. These compounds work by inhibiting the growth of the fungus that causes ringworm, Trichophyton rubrum. The antibacterial properties of Allium sativum creams, attributed to its flavonoid and terpenoid content, help prevent secondary infections and promote a healthy environment for wound healing. The antiseptic properties of Allium sativum creams also help reduce inflammation and promote the healing of skin lesions caused by ringworm.
remedies.image_alt: Allium sativum herbal creams for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of Allium sativum roots, 2 cups of coconut oil, and 1 cup of beeswax.
Dry the Allium sativum roots in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Grind the dried Allium sativum roots into a fine powder.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a saucepan over low heat, then add the powder and stir well.
Remove from heat, let cool and solidify, then store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol, two active constituents that have antifungal properties. These properties help to combat the fungal infection that causes ringworm. Thymol inhibits the growth of the fungus by interfering with its cell membrane, while carvacrol has antiseptic and antibacterial effects that prevent the spread of infection. Thymus vulgaris creams also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and itching associated with ringworm. By using thymus vulgaris creams, the fungus can be controlled, allowing the skin to heal and the infection to clear up.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, and 10 drops of Thyme essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until melted.
Add the olive oil and Thyme essential oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a silicone candy mold or a small tin container. Let it cool and solidify at room temperature.

Once solid, remove the Thymus vulgaris cream from the mold or tin and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. Apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains active constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help combat ringworm. Curcumin, in particular, has been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of fungi that cause ringworm. Demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin also have antifungal properties, making Curcuma longa creams effective in treating the infection. By reducing inflammation and killing the fungi, Curcuma longa creams can help clear up ringworm symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of aloe vera gel, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the turmeric powder to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Add the aloe vera gel and vitamin E oil. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before use. Apply to affected area 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica creams contains compounds like nimbin, nimondin, and azadirachtin, which are responsible for its antifungal properties. These compounds work by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells, thereby reducing the severity of ringworm symptoms. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of Azadirachta indica also help to soothe and calm affected skin, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, the cream's antifungal properties can help to prevent the spread of ringworm to other parts of the body and to others. The combination of these properties makes Azadirachta indica creams a potential effective treatment for ringworm.
remedies.image_alt: Azadirachta indica herbal creams for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Azadirachta indica leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1 cup of beeswax.
Dry the Azadirachta indica leaves in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the leaf powder with 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax melts and the mixture cools to form a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Origanum vulgare creams contains a compound called carvacrol, a powerful antifungal agent that helps combat ringworm. Carvacrol disrupts the fungal cell membrane, preventing the fungus from multiplying and causing infection. The cream also contains rosmarinic acid, a compound that has antimicrobial properties, which helps to reduce the growth of fungi and bacteria that can exacerbate ringworm. Additionally, the cream's high content of thymol, a phenolic compound, has antiseptic and antifungal properties that aid in the healing process of the affected skin. By applying Origanum vulgare creams to the affected area, the active constituents work together to combat the fungal infection and promote healthy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Origanum vulgare herbal creams for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Origanum vulgare leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Grind the dried Origanum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the ground Origanum vulgare powder to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help fight fungal infections like ringworm. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams can inhibit the growth of fungi and reduce inflammation, making it easier to heal the affected skin. Caffeic acid has antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting faster healing. The combination of these active constituents in Echinacea purpurea creams makes it a potential treatment for ringworm.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for ringworm
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and a double boiler.
Combine dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and coconut oil in a bowl. Let it sit for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the bowl every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Melt the coconut oil mixture and add shea butter and beeswax. Heat until everything is melted and well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool. Use the cream to treat ringworm twice a day.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent ringworm from forming, but its effectiveness is not proven. Some herbal teas, like tea tree oil and sage, have antifungal properties that can fight fungal infections, including ringworm. However, ringworm can still develop, and herbal tea is not a substitute for proper hygiene and treatment.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for ringworm are not a reliable treatment. Some might help soothe the skin, but they won't get rid of the fungus. Consuming them daily may not be safe, especially if the tea contains ingredients that interact with medications or have side effects. Be cautious and consult the label carefully.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas for ringworm can take a few weeks to show results. This is because they need time to fight the infection from the inside out. Some people may start to see improvement in a week or two, but it's usually around 3-6 weeks for the infection to clear up completely.
faq_time: For ringworm, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon, when your body is most active. This allows the tea's natural ingredients to help fight the fungal infection throughout the day. Drinking herbal tea at night might make it harder to fall asleep.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for ringworm. Some herbal teas, like St. John's Wort, can speed up how your body processes certain medicines, making them less effective. Others, like goldenseal, may interact with antibiotics, which are often used to treat ringworm. This can lead to unexpected side effects.
faq_pregnant: Herbal teas may help soothe ringworm symptoms, but it's essential to be cautious when pregnant. Some herbal teas contain ingredients that can stimulate the uterus or affect hormone levels, which could be risky during pregnancy. If you still want to try, look for teas made from gentle, non-stimulating herbs like calendula or chamomile.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding and having ringworm, it's essential to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs, like tea tree oil, may be helpful for ringworm, but they can also pass into breast milk and potentially affect the baby. It's best to check the ingredients and talk to someone you trust before consuming any herbal teas.
title_2: 10 Herbal Creams for Veterinarians to Overcome Ringworm
